---
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs
  render:
    - index.qmd
    - about.qmd
    - report.qmd
    - interactive-viz.qmd

website:
  title: "Predicting S&P 500 Returns with Macro-Financial Indicators"
  navbar:
    left:
      - text: "Home"
        href: index.qmd
      - text: "Final Report"
        href: report.qmd
      - text: "Interactive Visualizations"
        href: interactive-viz.qmd
      - text: "About"
        href: about.qmd
    right:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com/lijack032/JSC370-Project
        target: _blank

format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: right
    code-overflow: scroll
    css: styles.css

execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: true

jupyter: python3
---

# Project Overview

This project examines whether publicly available macro-financial indicators can meaningfully predict future S&P 500 returns. The analysis combines daily financial market data from Yahoo Finance with macroeconomic indicators from Federal Reserve Economic Data. The main research question is:

**Can macro-financial indicators meaningfully predict future S&P 500 returns, and do machine learning methods improve predictive accuracy relative to simpler benchmark and linear models?**

The project compares regression models for predicting the numerical size of future returns and classification models for predicting whether future returns are positive or non-positive. Models are evaluated across 1-day, 5-day, and 20-day forecast horizons.

## Data Sources

The dataset combines daily market indicators and macroeconomic indicators, including:

- S&P 500 Index
- Dow Jones Industrial Average
- 10-year and 1-year Treasury yields
- U.S. Dollar Index
- CBOE Volatility Index
- Crude oil prices
- Federal Funds Rate
- Consumer Price Index
- Unemployment rate
- Industrial production
- Consumer sentiment
- Credit spread

## Methods

The project uses a chronological training, validation, and test split to avoid look-ahead bias. The models include:

- Mean benchmark
- Majority-class benchmark
- Ordinary Least Squares
- Ridge Regression
- Lasso Regression
- Logistic Regression
- Random Forest
- XGBoost

Hyperparameters were tuned using Optuna. Regression models were evaluated using RMSE, MAE, and out-of-sample $R^2$. Classification models were evaluated using accuracy and ROC-AUC.

## Main Findings

The results suggest that exact return prediction is very difficult. Most regression models produced out-of-sample $R^2$ values close to zero or negative. The only positive regression result was the 1-day Random Forest model, but the improvement was very small.

Classification performance was somewhat more promising but still limited. Logistic Regression performed best across all three horizons, with the strongest result at the 20-day horizon. This suggests that macro-financial indicators may contain limited information for predicting medium-term market direction, but not enough for strong forecasting performance.

## Project Materials

- [Read the full final report](report.html)
- [View interactive visualizations](interactive-viz.html)
- [Download the PDF report](Report.pdf)
- [View the GitHub repository](https://github.com/lijack032/JSC370-Project)
